source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 11.3.3 Shutdown/suspend/hibernate (p. 207)

questions.q: Turn off (power off) the system with systemctl.
questions.why: systemctl poweroff replaces the old poweroff command and turns the system off.
systemctl reboot replaces the old reboot command.

questions.q: Suspend the system (the systemctl replacement for pm-suspend).
questions.why: systemctl suspend suspends the system (old pm-suspend).

questions.q: Hibernate the system (the systemctl replacement for pm-hibernate).
questions.why: systemctl hibernate hibernates the system (old pm-hibernate).

questions.q: Which systemctl command both hibernates AND suspends the system (the old pm-suspend-hybrid)?
questions.why: systemctl hybrid-sleep both hibernates and suspends, replacing pm-suspend-hybrid.
